"The old-time sheep days of Mon
tana are slowly passing away" he
cause the government lands are be-
ing sthrown open to settlement. Mon
tana is settling up very rapidly, and
each new settler takes just so much
land away from the sheep and cattle
men. The sheepmen use government
land largely, and as those tracts are
thrown open other grazing acreages
must be found for the sheep.


The Montana sheepmen made con-
siderable money the past two years.
owing To the poor condition of the
ranges sending up prices of meat and
wool. Some of the men who had
extra fine wool this year got as high
as % cents pound. That is doing
pretty we'll But the old-time days
are beginning to fade away with the
advent of settlement, and where the
shecpmen will ultimately go l do not
know"
